design_philosophy.core_loop: One-on-one fighting game with medieval theme; player selects character and engages in combat using directional inputs and button combinations to execute moves
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Direct player-versus-opponent combat with move execution timing and pattern recognition
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Medieval warrior combat with varied character movesets and fighting styles
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: TCRF explicitly describes it as 'wholly unremarkable' — suggests the game made minimal design innovations within the fighting game genre and played it safe with conventional 1v1 fighter design

progression.unlock_mechanism: Character selection roster with different fighters available
replayability_hooks: Multiple playable characters with distinct movesets
Arcade-style tournament/ladder progression implied by character roster

world_building_techniques: Medieval theming in character design and environment aesthetics
Character naming conventions suggesting fantasy world-building

faction_indicators: Character roster names suggest different regional or cultural backgrounds (Kerrel, Lang, Tesya, Shnouzer, Adrick, Autarch, Lord Gustoff)
danger_signals: Medieval combat setting with weapons (chain mace signature move for Lord Gustoff)
Competitive fighting game framework implies conflict-based scenarios
environmental_storytelling: Medieval fantasy visual theme communicated through character designs and setting

development_notes.unused_content: Ranged attacks (chain mace for Lord Gustoff and Shazzer/Shnouzer) documented in manuals and artwork but non-functional in actual gameplay — only AI opponents can perform these moves
development_notes.regional_differences: Character name changes between JP and US versions; character costume modifications for US release (Filea/Tesya given tights, likely for content concerns)
development_notes.development_artifacts: Japanese development text preserved in ROM containing move command labels (KAMAE, ZENNSINN, KOUTAI, various jump and attack move types)

sources_used: tcrf: Battle Blaze (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
